title: tephrosia punctata subsp. redheadii
intro: Tephrosia punctata subsp. redheadii, also known as Redhead's Tephrosia, is a plant species of the Fabaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Tephrosia punctata subsp. redheadii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are traditionally used to treat a range of ailments.
